Diacabs

Auto Index has moved 3x since COVID and in a general sense one would think that most of the auto ancillaries would have grown faster than the OEMs themselves...

The answer is both yes and no. Not all, but a few grew fast and they earned that auto ancillary premium. Interestingly, there were a few common patterns across each one of them.

Electricals compounded at 17%. Batteries at 8% (yes, the EV transition is not enough to save the day). Same sector label, completely different businesses.

After spending years tracking this space, here's the framework I'd use to separate the compounders from the capital traps:

1. Count the growth levers, not the growth rate

Companies pulling multiple levers simultaneously with new products, new geographies, new customers, acquisitions outperformed in every single cycle. Why? Because that's where the visibility on diversification of the revenue base comes from.

2. Not all content growth is equal

The most powerful dynamic of the decade was rising content per vehicle. But content growth has two very different flavours:

> Regulatory content (BS6, airbags, ABS, TPMS) - one-time. Vehicles comply, content is sold, growth rate normalises.

→ Structural content (premiumisation, EV adoption, connected car, digital clusters) - this compounds. It's driven by consumer preference, not a compliance deadline.

For the next 3–4 years, the question isn't "will auto volumes grow?" — it's "which companies are positioned on content categories that didn't even exist in FY16?"

3. The valuation anomaly most people are ignoring

Not all segments deserve the same multiple. Body & Glass is delivering 30% PAT CAGR. It will command a premium — why? Longevity of earnings irrespective of any sort of engine transition. Be it EV or ICE, both need body and glass.

And if you're paying a premium for a story that may have missed in the past or carries any sort of transition risk, then one has to be completely aware of that. At those multiples, even in-line delivery is arithmetically a de-rating quarter.

Bottom line:

The sector's balance sheet is the healthiest in a decade with Net Debt/EBITDA at 0.18x. The consensus FY26–28 PAT CAGR is 21%.

This sector rewards the diversified over the concentrated, the structural content grower over the compliance beneficiary, and the consistent deliverer over the compelling storyteller.

One has to find a business bottoms-up in this sector that has such characteristics one that has created a competitive moat, is diversifying revenue streams (organically or inorganically), and where valuations can be argued upon.

Source - Equirus

The rupee’s slide against US dollar has sharpened over the past 2 years.

From Rs 83.35 per $1 in April 2024, the exchange rate moved past Rs 95 per dollar by May 2026.

At its weakest point in this period, the rupee touched Rs 96.84 per $1 on May 20, before recovering slightly to Rs 95.44 per $1 on May 26.

This means the dollar became about 16% costlier for anyone converting rupees into dollars — affecting import costs, overseas travel, foreign education payments, and companies with dollar-linked expenses.

A weaker rupee can also add pressure on India’s import bill, especially for commodities such as crude oil, while making exports relatively more competitive.

text:
Is the Market Peaking or Entering a Super-Cycle?

Is the $1.78tn SpaceX IPO a market top or the launch of an investment super-cycle? While some fear a bubble, macro indicators suggest we are at the beginning of a massive global capex boom.

Multi-sector data shows massive acceleration. Global spending across AI, clean energy, and defense hit $6.9tn in 2025. It is projected to reach $10tn this year and scale to $16tn by 2030.

Capital is rotating into domestic infrastructure and manufacturing resilience. Reshoring trade-exposed goods entirely to the US requires $2tn in upgraded factories and facilities.

🎯 Bottom Line: With $8tn in money market funds acting as dry powder, pullbacks are tactical buying opportunities. We are entering a multi-year, capex-driven "tripolar world" expansion.

Gold Digital Trading to Go Live on NSE

NSE is set to fully launch its Electronic Gold Receipts (EGR) segment in the next ten weeks.

Now you’ll be able to buy and sell gold just like shares.

Key Highlights-:

-Only guaranteed 995 and 999 (24-carat) purity.

-Facility to purchase from 1 kilo down to just 100 milligrams.

-Timing: Trading from 9:00 AM to 11:30 PM.
-Gold will be securely stored in SEBI-regulated lockers.

3% GST will apply on physical delivery,
but 0% GST for trading on the exchange.

However, retail volumes may take a bit of time to ramp up as major brokers are enabling it gradually.
Are you ready to trade in digital gold?

HDFC AMC and NAM fund performance trends have been incredibly strong over the past two years, with both seeing flow market shares rise. 

 The same is also true for ICICI Pru AMC (albeit listed later).  

FY23-26 saw performance trends and brand both favor the larger players.  

Brand is relevant for retail flows. 

If performance trends do not inch back up soon, the ‘performance + brand’ combo could become a ‘performance v. brand’ dynamic. 

low market share gains could be tested, at a time when equity returns and flow momentum are not as rosy as they once were.

Syrma - PCB Foray 

• Entering India's ₹80,000+ Cr PCB import substitution opportunity via a 75:25 JV with Shinhyup Korea

• Building a 13 lakh sq. ft. PCB facility on 24 acres in Andhra Pradesh, with operations targeted by Mar'27 

• ₹1,600 Cr phased capex over 3-5 years, supported by ₹765 Cr ECMS incentives (~50% reimbursement)
  >> Phase 1: 1.2 Mn sq. m. capacity (720K multilayer + 480K single-layer PCB)
  >> Phase 2: HDI & Flex PCBs

• Completes Syrma's electronics value chain: PCB → PCBA → Box Build → System

• India's PCB market is projected to grow from $6.3 Bn to $24.7 Bn by 2033

Jefferies report interesting read. Basically saying one thing clearly: AI data center demand is not the problem. Physical supply is the bottleneck. The world wants far more data center capacity than the ecosystem can build and commission fast enough. The cleanest theme: AI infrastructure bottlenecks and their beneficiaries. The best names are the ones sitting at real choke points where demand is exploding but supply cannot scale easily. Key buckets: Cooling:
[$VRT](https://x.com/search?q=%24VRT&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$CARR](https://x.com/search?q=%24CARR&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$TT](https://x.com/search?q=%24TT&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$JCI](https://x.com/search?q=%24JCI&src=cashtag_click)
Power equipment / transformers / grid:
[$GEV](https://x.com/search?q=%24GEV&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$ETN](https://x.com/search?q=%24ETN&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$WCC](https://x.com/search?q=%24WCC&src=cashtag_click)
EPC / construction:

,
[$MTZ](https://x.com/search?q=%24MTZ&src=cashtag_click)
,

Data center capacity owners / developers:

Zee Eyes Gold Rush with Big Ad Rate Push; Official FIFA World Cup 2026 Broadcaster Pitches ₹16,600 Per 10 Seconds Unified Rate — 10% Above 2022 Edition

The Ad Rate — What Zee Is Offering
Zee Entertainment — official broadcaster of the 2026 FIFA World Cup — offering a unified ad rate of ~₹16,600 per 10 seconds combining TV and digital

Rate is an increase of little over 10% over the 2022 edition price

Reliance-backed Viacom18 (now part of JioStar) had charged ~₹15,000 unified ad rate during the last World Cup four years ago

For comparison, Premier League — which has a sizeable viewer base in India — draws ad rates of ~₹10,000 per 10 seconds

RBI's Hidden Liquidity Play

ECB and FCCB flows plunged 30% in FY26 to $42.9B. The RBI is keeping repo rates steady at 5.25% while managing supply shocks, forcing a structural pivot toward alternative dollar mobilization.

The consensus treats this status quo as a pause, missing the liquidity trap. By absorbing hedging costs for FCNR-B deposits, the central bank is aggressively subsidizing domestic deposit growth

This regulatory alpha triggers a regime shift. Swapping external debt for domestic credit transmission protects corporate margins from overseas de-rating, even as CPI inflation peaks at 5.9%.

Bottom Line: Stop watching the repo rate headline. The real alpha is in the backend forex reforms designed to cap domestic yields and guarantee credit growth without bleeding the currency.

RBI INCREASES OVERSEAS INVESTMENT LIMIT FOR INDIVIDUAL RESIDENTS UNDER OPI TO 24% FROM 10% IN INDIAN LISTED COMPANIES

Paint industry commentary from Indigo Paints gives a very interesting insight into the current demand trend

Management highlighted that the paint industry had gone through a fairly long weak cycle, with almost 8 consecutive quarters of muted demand. After nearly 2 years of softness, demand started improving meaningfully from Nov’25 onwards.

But just when demand was recovering, the Middle east conflict war created fresh supply chain disruption. Mar'26 saw a sharp spike in raw material costs & tight availability of critical inputs.

The most important insight is around volume growth versus value growth.

Indigo management pointed out that most paint companies have been reporting volume growth significantly higher than value growth for the last 7-8 quarters.

This clearly suggests that economy & budget products are growing much faster than premium products for most paint companies.

Management said that in their case, value growth has generally been higher than volume growth across categories.

Management also said that they have been gradually premiumising their product portfolio over the last 2-3 years.

Overall paint sector is seeing demand recovery after a weak phase.

HDFC Mutual Fund has imposed temporary restrictions on lump sum subscriptions for HDFC Gold ETF and HDFC Gold ETF Fund of Funds (FoF).

Direct lump sum subscriptions of ₹25 crore or more in HDFC Gold ETF are restricted effective June 8, 2026

Lump sum and switch-in transactions for the HDFC Gold ETF FOF are capped at 10 lakh per PAN per month, effective June 5, 2026

Citi characterizes the impact on HDFC AMC's overall revenues as negligible, estimating the contribution from incremental gold ETF flows at less than 1%

Restrictions were implemented in response to broader economic and market conditions to manage inflows efficiently

The firm continues to monitor the redemption trajectory as a key indicator of market sentiment

@Stockinfo333

MOTILAL OSWAL ON INDIAN ECONOMY 

Economy Note - FY27 Consumption Risk and opportunities

El Nino (92-96% probability during core monsoon) + West Asia energy shock = a convergence not priced by markets. 

MOFSL sees FY27 CPI at 5.7% vs RBI's 4.6% — a 110-120bp gap.

Rural India bears the brunt (food is 42% of rural CPI vs 30% urban). The earnings hit lands in corporate results Q1/Q2FY27 well before it shows in GDP prints. 

Urban premium consumption is the hide; while 

Tractors, Entry 2W, Rural FMCG, and MFIs are the pain trade.

Key Monitorable ahead is Reservoir level to assess how Rabi crops will be.

Jefferies on Adani Ports
Buy, TP Rs 2160

Adani Ports remains confident of achieving its 1bnt cargo target by 2030 (16% CAGR vs JEFe’s 13%), driven by organic growth in domestic ports and existing international port capacity. Integrated logistics is a growth enabler for ports as well.

Capital allocation remains a key focus area with 100 bps annual RoCE expansion target over FY26-31E vs. JEFe 60 bps
Believe strong cash flows enable growth capex, & potential deleveraging to coexist

CLSA on Bharti Airtel
O-P, TP Rs 2310

IPO for Airtel Money, Bharti Airtel’s Africa mobile money business, is scheduled for 2H26.

Airtel Money reportedly could raise US$1.5-2bn at a potential valuation of US$10bn, up four fold from 2021, implying c.60% of Airtel Africa’s market cap
Airtel Money’s FY26 revenue was up 36% YoY to US$1.4bn, while Ebitda increased 31% YoY to US$689m.

Airtel Money penetration is low at 29% of Airtel Africa’s own mobile subscribers of 184m as Nigeria has yet to ramp up; thus, strong growth will likely continue.

Airtel Money business contributes c.20% of region, & Africa accounts for 25% of Bharti Airtel’s consolidated operations.

Capacity & model cycle to drive growth

Asia Conf takeaways

UVs: M&M is optimistic on demand outlook of mid-to-high teens volume growth for SUVs in FY27E. Most of models have high demand, but supplies have been affected over past
two months

M&M expects this issue to be resolved in coming months
M&M expects SUV capacity to expand from 64.5k/mth (56.5k ICE+ 8k EVs ) to 68k by Sep-26 & 82k by Mar-26 (70k ICE + 12k EVs)

Launch of NU-IQ platform will be supported by this capacity expansion

Co plans to launch 10 ICEs and 6BEVs over FY27-31E

Nagpur plant will eventually have capacity of 500k SUVs & 100k tractors which will commence operation in 2028

Goldman Sachs on Titan : 

Maintains BUY rating; target price of ₹5,400
Axis Capital-Sharper focus on market share plus volumes led by aggressive pricing and inhouse strategy has led Voltas gain ~170bps market share YoY in Apr’26

Goldman Sachs on Adani Ports and SEZ: 

Maintains BUY rating; raises target price to ₹1,870

CLSA on Titan
O-P, TP Rs 5249

Reaffirming a 20% Cagr, targeting 2x FY26 revenue & 1.9x EBIT for jewellery segment by FY30

Management noted resilient consumer sentiment

Co’s portfolio-led approach across categories is enabling premiumisation and strengthening brand equity.

Given strong positioning to benefit from rising affluence and premium trends, maintain O-P

HSBC on Titan
Buy, TP Rs 5250

Titan’s FY30 ambition implies 19% CAGR each in consol revenues / EBIT and TMZ (Tanishq, Mia, Zoya) at 19% / 16%
Jewellery business growth, following strong three years, looks slightly aggressive, but raise expectation to 17% CAGR

FY27/28e EPS up 1%/2%; beyond short-term regulatory uncertainty, investment case is compelling

@Stocksip

Jefferies on Titan
Hold, TP Rs 4800

Titan targets c20% revenue/Ebit CAGR over FY26-30, led by SSS, network expansion, formalisation, share gain, premiumisation, etc., notwithstanding ST concerns on macro, inflation & high gold prices.

Cross-sell is a lever with a 50mn customer base and an improving digital/CRM platform.

International also adds to growth
Find Titan mgmt & execution best-in-class, regulatory overhang tempers outlook

Channel Checks Suggest Q1FY27 Could Be Softer Than Current Expectations

Expects USD Revenue Cuts Of 50-100 bps, Though EPS May Be Protected By Forex Benefits

Deal Ramp-ups & Signings Delayed Due To Geopolitical Uncertainty

A Soft Q1 Could Make FY27 Growth Targets More Challenging

MORGAN STANLEY ON TITAN

OVERWEIGHT, TARGET 5182

Jewellery demand saw some impact after PM's comments, but rebounded thereafter

Titan continues to promote gold exchange

Jewellery (TMZ) revenues to grow at a 19% F26-F30 CAGR, with a 16% EBIT CAGR

Market share gains to continue (4.5% F19, 8.5% F26, and target 11% by F30)

CITI ON GAIL

ACCUMULATE, TARGET 195

Transmission volumes have recovered from the lows of March 2026

Mgmt. is confident of meeting its gas marketing EBIT guidance of Rs40-45bn in FY27E

LPG segment is expected to deliver improved profitability

Petchem plant is still just running at half load, losses could narrow given higher realisations

⚡️ Citi on Power Equipment Stocks

🏆 Top Picks

• Hitachi Energy — Buy | TP ₹46,700 | EPS CAGR 43% (FY26-30)

• GE Vernova T&D — Buy | TP ₹6,200

• CG Power — Buy | TP ₹1,100 | EPS CAGR 33% (FY26-30)

• Siemens Energy — Neutral | TP ₹4,000 | EPS CAGR 23% (FY25-30)

CITI ON LUPIN

ACCUMULATE, TARGET PRICE 2540

Confident of maintaining ~25% EBITDA margin in FY27 as well as in FY28E

India business is expected to grow at 1.2-1.3x IPM in FY27

US sales are expected to be US$1-1.1bn in FY27E, followed by further growth in FY28E

Net debt is unlikely to exceed 2x EBITDA even in the event of an acquisition

CITI ON ICICI LOMBARD

Expect gross premium growth to be 100-200 bps higher than industry average

Competitive pricing in commercial lines

IFRS can lead to marginal improvement in profitability in short-term

Focus on gradual improvement in combined ratio

CITI ON MAX HEALTHCARE

ACCUMULATE, TARGET 1330

Company is targeting 10-15% annual bed additions, primarily brownfield-led (~60%)

Execution delays (~4-6 months) are temporary and not margin-dilutive

Near-term margins may remain impacted by pre-hiring and commissioning costs

Insurance related issues are largely behind with the mgmt

CITI ON SUN PHARMA

ACCUMULATE, TARGET 2180

In Organon specialty portfolio, VTAMA and Emgality are witnessing strong growth

Organon's strong presence in China provides a meaningful opportunity

Sun can also commercialize its own products across markets such as China and Korea

Biosimilars and established brands offer additional growth levers

CITI ON GRASIM

ACCUMULATE, TARGET 3600

Paints-price hikes, cost pressures, uncertainty beyond 1Q, key priorities for Birla Opus

Capital allocation-focus on core businesses now following two new businesses

Stronger sequential 1Q for VSF and Chemicals

Paint revenue market share likely crossed ~10% in Mar26

There is no elevator to success.
You have to take the stairs.

#sector

Citi On India electrical Equipment

Power grids are emerging as the key bottleneck to energy transition. 

BNEF expects sing renewable penetration, data centers and industrial growth to drive ~US$15tn in global T&D capex over CY25-50. 

We see India as uniquely positioned (it manufactures ~80% of the global T&D products) to benefit 
from a large domestic transmission buildout, accelerating HVDC adoption, favourable localization policies, and growing export opportunities. 

Initiate Buy on Hitachi Energy/POWERIND (TP Rs46,700), GVTD (TP Rs6,200) and CGPI (TP R$1,100), and Neutral on Siemens Energy/ENRIN (TP Rs4,000).

**How BioPharm Strengthens Indegene**

**Acquisition Overview**
- Deal Value Up To $106M
- Largest Revenue Acquisition Yet
- Serves 17 Top Pharma Clients
- Expands Commercialization Capabilities

**What BioPharm Brings**

**HCP Media & AdTech**
- Programmatic HCP Engagement Platform
- Omnichannel Media Capabilities
- Push And Pull Marketing Models
- Direct Brand-Level Relationships

**Tandem Data Platform**
- 3M+ HCP Profiles
- 300M+ Campaign Exposure Events
- 15M+ Verified Engagements
- Built Over Two Decades

**Data Advantage**
- Physician Profiles Expand Sharply
- Strong Oncology Coverage Added
- Rare Disease Data Added
- Ophthalmology Coverage Strengthened

**Completing The Commercial Funnel**

**Upstream Layer**
- CultHealth Drives Creative Strategy
- Tectonic Supports Brand Development

**Execution Layer**
- ECS Manages Content Production
- Campaign Execution Capabilities

**Data & Media Layer**
- BioPharm Enables Media Activation
- Addressable HCP Targeting Added
- Programmatic Campaign Delivery
- Measurement And Optimization Tools

**Strategic Benefits**
- Full-Funnel Commercialization Achieved
- No Structural Capability Gaps
- Cross-Sell Opportunities Increase
- Brand Team Access Strengthens

**Competitive Moat**
- Proprietary Data Difficult To Replicate
- Supports AI And Analytics
- Enhances Physician Intelligence
- Creates Long-Term Differentiation

**Integration Progress**
- Support Transition Underway
- Omnichannel Wins Already Visible
- Cross-Selling Activity Started
- FY26 Integration Remains Key

**Key Takeaway**
- BioPharm Adds Revenue And Capability
- Tandem Platform Is Core Asset
- Completes Data And Media Layer
- Strengthens Indegene's Pharma Ecosystem

Why do companies like HFCL and Sterlite Tech run up like crazy? Read the AI fibre intensity part below.

The binding constraint to watch: not demand — it is power availability, grid connectivity, and cooling retrofit capacity. Whoever removes those bottlenecks captures disproportionate economics. This is precisely why the equipment layer, not the operator layer, is where the alpha sits.
